calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-21-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Schindler for Adam Wong (Petitioner).

Mr. Schindler provided the case history and addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-13-2014 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-05-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Schindler for Adam Wong (Petitioner).

Mr. Schindler described the Third Street property, and presented oral argument to support his position. The Court finds that the assets of Katherine are all community property, that one half of the assets will be transferred to the Trust and the other half will remain in Katherine's estate until something happens in Justin's estate. The Court also noted that Justin's estate is not before the Court. The accounting petition is granted (subject to receipt of a revised order that complies with the Court's ruling). Mr. Schindler is to prepare the revised order.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-20-2013 Continued To Jun-03-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-22-2013 Continued To May-20-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Schindler for Adam Wong.

Ms. Schindler stated that he received the Probate Examiner's notes regarding the Estate of Justin Wong (Husband), that Katherine died first, and that there was a Trust but that the property was not inventoried early on. Mr. Schindler also stated that it is his guess that they found documents of accounts that were in her name that were invested, that they chose to do this petition instead of a hegstad petition and that he got most of the accounts into the Trust. The Court eplained that they need to separate the assets and charactarize the property as community or separate property, and show what can go by way of the will into the Wife's Trust and the Husband's Trust. Mr. Schindler argued that they only have the one Trust, that the Wife's assets in this probate ought to be transferred to the Trust, that all of the assets seem to belong in the Trust and that he does not know if they can come up with any more information. The accounting petition was continued to May 20, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to file a declaration.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
